Now, all of these things I've been speaking about, they all make up a body of psychology you may have heard of called Neuro Linguistic Program or NLP for short. And NLP, what it does basically is it deals with specifically how people make change in their lives and how to actually make sure those changes last. And when NLP is used correctly, it can be an incredibly potent tool for making changes in your own life and specifically in this context when it comes to learning how to manage your own state through the process of anchoring. And just so you know, I like NLP to almost like an incredibly sharp sword. Like there was this movie I loved called Cratching Tiger Hidden Dragon and they had this magical sword, right? And the sword was, you know, invented thousands of years ago, a lost art, no one could have this sword anymore. If the wrong person wielded the sword, it wasn't all that powerful. But in the hands of a hardly trained expert, the sword becomes an almost magical weapon. Well, the same is true of NLP. NLP can be incredibly effective when used by the right person. And also if someone shows you how to integrate certain aspects of NLP into your own life. That's a very, very rare thing. I had to benefit myself of actually training directly with the person who founded the body of psychology of NLP, a guy, a man named Dr. Richard Vanler. And I spent a lot of time with Dr. Vanler, thankfully, and he blew me away. And what I came away with is this. In many ways, NLP is almost about stating the obvious about the world around us, the way it operates, how we make sense of the world. But there are two distinct aspects of NLP that I found to be unbelievably effective at helping me make changes in other people, specifically salespeople. And the first one is this process of anchoring and how it relates to state management. That's an NLP based process, which I actually then take it up another level by some of my own experiences using a sense of smell and we'll get there. Okay. So it's very, very powerful. And the second process is in the changing of some of your core beliefs. You actually take some of your old limiting beliefs that have been holding you back from achieving the success you truly can't believe. And we root them out using an NLP process and we actually replace them with empowering beliefs of your choice that allow you to essentially propel yourself to massive success in sales. So the question is, how do you actually train your body to go into the states that you want to be in more often, meaning those empowered states? The answer is very simple. You learn to do it through a process called anchoring. And what anchoring means essentially is to lock in or to deeply anchor in as the phrase goes, a state that serves you so deeply in fact that it almost comes part of who you are. So you can then trigger that anchor or fire off the anchor as the phrase goes, which is the same as saying triggering a peak state at will. To put it simply, all an anchor is, it's basically a learned association between a specific state and a specific trigger. Let me give you a quick example. You've probably heard of Pavlov's dogs, right? So an experiment done many years ago by a Russian psychologist named Pavlov. And he trained these dogs to salivate every time they heard a dinner bell, even if in the absence of food. Now the way he did this was by simultaneously ringing a dinner bell every time he fed them a piece of meat. And he did this again and again and again, more than a hundred times in fact. So he fed them some meat rang a bell, fed the meat rang a bell, fed meat rang a bell, right? And eventually he could just ring the bell and the dogs would start to salivate even in the absence of meat. It was the anticipation of the coming meat that caused the response. Okay? So the bell in this case is what's called the anchor. And when you ring the bell, it's called firing off your anchor. And the good news is that all we're not dogs, we can use this process for ourselves to trigger any particular state we want, regardless of how we're feeling at that moment. In other words, we can set anchors for ourselves for things like confidence and clarity and certainty. So even if we're not actually feeling those things, we can actually fire off our anchor and trigger those empowering states at will. And the best news of all about this, by the way, is that unlike drugs, which you typically need more and more of to get the same effect, it's the exact opposite when it comes to anchoring. You see, when you fire off an anchor, the more you do it, the easier it gets to fire it off and the more powerful the response is. So that being said, let's cut to the live footage now and I'll show you exactly how to set an anchor for yourself. And I'll see you in a few minutes. So when I started getting into neurolinguistic programming with Banner, the basic way of it's called anchoring is where you actually learn to trigger an emotion that you want by setting what's called an anchor. Let's say I want to, at any time I want, get into a peak state of certainty. I want to feel certain. So what I do is I go back to a moment in my life and I get myself feeling absolutely certain about what happened five years ago or 10 years ago. For me, here's my anchor. I go back to the night that I invented the straight line. It was that night that I drew that straight line, the board said, don't you guys get it? Every sale is the same. There's an open, there's a closed. And I remember it looking out at the people and I go back to that moment and I imagine what I was wearing, how I was feeling and I closed my eyes and I actually squeezed my fist so I feel my own thing and I was digging in and I say, yes. And I just go back and I get myself feeling as I felt. I look out to my own eyes and I see what I was seeing. The people seeing it from me, hear what I was saying. I watched their heads nodding and like, oh my God, like I was in full. I get that unbelievable, I get myself that feeling of being in charge and knowing. I knew I cracked the code. I knew that night. I just, I said, I got it. I know how to train these people. I can do it. I'm going to be rich. And I went on and on for hours and everyone was writing it down and writing stuff down. And I just, and I get myself really worked up into that moment. I say, yes. And I say, yeah. And I walk around in a room. I said, I'll be before I give a seminar. I'll say, I'll say, yes. And I go back and I say, act as if and I'll smack my hand into mine. I'll create a noise from my spine alone. Then I'll go into one of my own language patterns that I had. Act if you're wealthy, man rich, or you'll become rich. And I thought, and I get myself, everyone see what I do? I get myself pumped up. And I say, yes. I see, and I smack my own, my anchor for certainty. We all have an angle. We're feeling certain. Some people do this. Some people do this. Some people do this. I do this. Everyone's different. Okay. And I say, yes. And then what I do is I take this thing here. It's this really pungent smell that I found. And I go, that's right. And I go, yes. And I get this wild smell. And as soon as that smell touches my brain, boom, I'm back to where I was that first night when I invented this thing. I actually added this on afterwards. The beautiful thing about your brain, you can change and add things on. You can change your memory. You can strengthen a memory. You can make it better than it was. And unlike drugs, which become less and less over time, this becomes more powerful. The more you do it, the stronger the anchor becomes. Everyone get that? The more it's, who has ever been in a car and got pulled over by the cops? Raise your hand. When you first heard that, what did you do? Your heart was beating before your mind even knew what happened. Instantly your blood, that's an anchor. So what I have done now by repetition and training, I've actually got to the point now, well, I could be sitting in my room in a low energy, neutral state and you go, yes. Well, bam, I'm absolutely certain. So I say to myself, here's what I do. I say, well, I have two options. Either I can A, give a seminar, give a public keynote all over the world, right, for companies. So I can do that and walk on stage and hope that I'm going to feel really good that day. I can give a seminar like this and hope I'm on that day. I hope I'm having a good day or I could take out any possibility and I trigger my anchor. Everyone get that? See my commitment to excellence in my life is I'm not going to leave it up to chance. I'm going to tip the odds in my favor. There's so much in sales guys that we can't control. We can't control the quality of our leads. We can try, but there's going to be bad leads. You can't control what's happening in the stock market. You can't control what's happening with the financial product you're selling. You can't control almost anything in this world, but what you can control is the state that you're in before you pick up that phone or before you walk in the door. So I say to myself, why leave it up to chance? I want to tip the odds in my favor. So I anchor myself into a peak state of certainty, of clarity, of confidence, of courage every single time.
